results.possibleIdentity: Henry Moss (often referred to in records as Moss Henry) was a reliability engineer active during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed by the Airborne Instruments Laboratory (AIL), a division of Cutler-Hammer based in Deer Park, New York. His technical work focused on reliability engineering, the analysis and design of precision mechanisms, and the development of electronic chassis. Moss is historically recognized for his association with the non-profit organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), which facilitated collaborations between technical professionals and artists. Through E.A.T., Moss became a long-term collaborator and 'surrogate' for the American artist Bruce Conner. In a notable conceptual art project in 1967, Conner recruited Moss to act as his double and stand-in for public appearances, including a campaign where Moss ran for the San Francisco Board of Supervisors in the artist's place.
